flashcards.back: Bitcoin is the world's first decentralized digital currency and the first practical application of blockchain technology, introduced by Satoshi Nakamoto in 2008.
The double-spending problem is the risk that digital currency can be spent twice. Bitcoin solved this through its blockchain and proof-of-work consensus mechanism.
The Bitcoin blockchain is a public, immutable, and append-only ledger that records all Bitcoin transactions in a chain of blocks secured by cryptographic hashes.
Bitcoin mining is the process where miners use computational power to validate transactions, add new blocks to the blockchain, and are rewarded with newly created bitcoins.
Nakamoto Consensus is Bitcoin's consensus mechanism that combines Proof-of-Work with the longest chain rule to achieve agreement on the state of the blockchain in a decentralized network.
